title: Passerina pilosa
taxonomy: Passerina pilosa is a species of plant in the family Passifloraceae. It belongs to the genus Passerina, which is a group of flowering plants. Passerina pilosa is native to parts of North and South America. The classification of Passerina pilosa is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Malpighiales, family Passifloraceae, genus Passerina, species P. pilosa. intro: Passerina pilosa, also known as Passionflower, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its distinctive flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, with reported health benefits including reduced stress and improved sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Passerina pilosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Passerina pilosa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
